description: Enterprise churn signal with multiple symptoms
input: Sarah from enterprise sales pinged me — Acme Corp is threatening to churn. They say onboarding took 3 weeks instead of the promised 1, their admin couldn't figure out how to set up SSO, and two of their power users say the bulk export feature is 'broken'. The CSM is also flagging that we've had 3 similar complaints this quarter from other mid-market accounts.
notes: Should identify onboarding/time-to-value as core problem, not export feature

description: Classic analytics signal with no root cause
input: Users are dropping off at step 3 of our onboarding flow. We don't know why. It's been happening for about 3 weeks. Drop-off rate went from 12% to 34%.
notes: Should not suggest solutions. Assumption should be about cause, not fix.

description: Sales-driven feature request with business context
input: Three enterprise prospects in the last month asked for role-based access controls during demos. We lost one deal to a competitor who had it. Legal is also starting to ask about it for our own compliance. Current workaround is sharing admin credentials which is obviously bad.
notes: Affected should cover both external (prospects) and internal (legal/compliance)

description: Low-quality input — should still produce useful output with low confidence
input: The product feels slow and clunky. Users don't like it.
notes: confidence should be low. missing_context should be populated.

input: Engineering wants to rewrite the data pipeline for performance. Sales says customers don't care about performance, they want more integrations. Support is getting 50 tickets/week about slow reports. CEO just came back from a conference and wants us to add AI features.
notes: Should identify the underlying problem (slow reports) not get distracted by CEO/AI request
